name: Browse AI
description: Browse.ai web scraping and monitoring robot platform. List and manage robots, run scraping tasks, and retrieve extracted data.

tools.name: status
tools.description: Check Browse AI API and task queue status. Use as a zero-argument connection test.

tools.name: list_robots
tools.description: Retrieve a list of all robots in your Browse.ai account. Returns robot IDs, names, creation dates, and input parameter configurations. Use this to discover available robots before running tasks.

tools.name: get_robot
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific robot by its ID. Returns the robot's name, configuration, input parameters, and creation date.

robot_id.description: The unique identifier of the robot to retrieve.

tools.name: run_robot
tools.description: Start a new task by running a robot with the specified input parameters. The robot will navigate to the target URL and extract data based on its configuration. Returns the created task object with its ID for tracking progress.

robot_id.description: The unique identifier of the robot to run.

inputParameters.description: Input parameters for the robot, typically including 'originUrl' (the target URL to scrape) and any other robot-specific parameters.

originUrl.description: The target URL for the robot to navigate to and scrape.

tools.name: get_task
tools.description: Retrieve the results and status of a specific task. Returns task status (successful, failed, in-progress), captured text data, screenshots, timestamps, and a debug video URL. Poll this endpoint to check if a running task has completed.

robot_id.description: The unique identifier of the robot that owns the task.

task_id.description: The unique identifier of the task to retrieve results for.

tools.name: list_tasks
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of tasks for a specific robot. Supports filtering by status and date range. Returns task IDs, statuses, timestamps, and extracted data summaries.

robot_id.description: The unique identifier of the robot to list tasks for.

page.description: Page number for pagination. Defaults to 1.

pageSize.description: Number of tasks per page. Defaults to 10.

tools.name: bulk_run_robot
tools.description: Start a bulk run for a robot with many input parameter sets. Use this for large URL/listing batches instead of starting tasks one by one.

robot_id.description: The unique identifier of the robot to run.

inputParameters.description: Array of input parameter objects, one task per item.

tools.name: get_bulk_run
tools.description: Retrieve a bulk run by ID, including its status and child task results.

robot_id.description: The unique identifier of the robot.

bulk_run_id.description: The unique identifier of the bulk run.

tools.name: list_monitors
tools.description: List monitors configured for a robot.

tools.name: create_monitor
tools.description: Create a monitor for a robot. The request body follows Browse AI's monitor schema and usually includes inputParameters and schedule settings.

tools.name: get_monitor
tools.description: Retrieve one monitor for a robot.

tools.name: update_monitor
tools.description: Update one monitor for a robot.

tools.name: delete_monitor
tools.description: Delete one monitor for a robot.

tools.name: list_webhooks
tools.description: List webhooks configured for a robot.

tools.name: create_webhook
tools.description: Create a robot webhook that Browse AI calls after robot task events.
